methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Vertical Aerospace is a public-stage transportation company that has raised over $400M. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of five private-tier transportation peers—Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—with no public comparables identified in the same narrow segment.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. The transportation sector contains only five same-stage peers overall, and filtering by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints necessary for a reliable median. Applying an all-sector median would conflate meaningfully different business models and risk profiles within transportation, introducing material noise into the analysis.

The absence of a sufficient narrow peer set and zero public comparables substantially constrains valuation precision. Without triangulation across multiple methods, an evidence-based range cannot be defensibly constructed from the data provided. To support a valuation conclusion, additional comparable data—either public markets benchmarks in adjacent mobility segments, or transaction multiples from the private transportation financing markets—would be required. The current dataset is insufficient to establish a point estimate or range with analytical confidence.
